title: Expose sync retry backoff (next retry time) in daemon/CLI status

description:
**Problem**
Operators need offline tool to verify and repair WAL/index when daemon is stopped.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §5.2 (offline repair, online vs offline boundary)

**Design**
- CLI: `bd store fsck --store-id <id>`
- Scans wal/<ns> segments, validates headers, frame CRCs, sha256, prev_sha continuity, per-(ns,origin) contiguity
- Cross-checks wal.sqlite offsets if present
- Emits deterministic FsckReport schema (compatible with admin.doctor later)
- `bd store fsck --repair`: truncate corrupted tails, quarantine mid-file segments (move to quarantine/), rebuild wal.sqlite

**Acceptance**
- [ ] bd store fsck validates WAL segments offline
- [ ] --repair truncates tails and quarantines bad segments
- [ ] Report uses a stable FsckReport schema for later admin.doctor reuse
- [ ] Tests with golden corpus fixtures

**Files:** src/cli/commands/store.rs, src/daemon/wal/fsck.rs (new)
title: Phase 3: bd store fsck offline verification tool

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Metadata preservation tests assert recomputed post-migration checksums in `meta.json`.
- [ ] Existing `root_slug` and `last_write_stamp` preservation assertions remain in place.
- [ ] Regression tests fail if migrated `meta.json` keeps stale pre-migration checksum values.
description:
**Problem**
The migration coverage around metadata preservation still misses checksum correctness. The current tests prove `root_slug` and `last_write_stamp` survive migration when `meta.json` already exists, but they do not assert that the rewritten `meta.json` recomputes store checksums against the post-migration strict snapshot. A regression could preserve the visible fields while leaving stale pre-migration checksums behind.

**Files**: crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs
design:
**Design**
Strengthen the metadata-preservation tests so they also pin checksum correctness.

1. In the existing local-only and remote-parented metadata preservation tests, read back the migrated strict snapshot bytes.
2. Recompute `StoreChecksums` from the rewritten state/tombstones/deps/notes blobs.
3. Assert the migrated `meta.json` carries those recomputed checksums, in addition to preserving `root_slug` and `last_write_stamp`.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 4cd04383: migration coverage now asserts recomputed meta.json checksums alongside preserved root_slug and last_write_stamp, so post-migration metadata truth stays pinned instead of only checking selected fields. --repo /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs

title: Migration follow-up: assert recomputed checksums when preserving existing meta fields
